business.headline: Parle Industries hits 5% upper circuit again as investors chase wrong Parle stockParle Industries shares hit the upper circuit for a third straight session after the Modi-Meloni Melody video went viral. The rally reflected confusion over the Parle name, even though Melody toffees are made by unlisted Parle Products.

Sensex, Nifty open higher as crude stays near $105; banks, financials lead gainsThe BSE Sensex was up 336.46 points at 75,519.82 around 9:26 am, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 86.30 points to trade at 23,741.00.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will markets rise on easing crude prices or stay volatile?Dalal Street is set for a near-flat start as crude hovers near $105 and traders watch US-Iran talks. Foreign selling, mixed technical signals and domestic buying are keeping the market range-bound.

Not Saudi, not US: India's newest major oil supplier may surprise youVenezuela became India's third-largest crude supplier in May, overtaking Saudi Arabia and the United States. The shift shows how lower prices and regional supply disruptions are reshaping India's crude sourcing.

Volatile markets are testing investors. Here's where smart money is movingFrom tensions in the Middle East to growing fragmentation in global trade and continued pressure on commodity prices, multiple headwinds have battered investor sentiment worldwide. However, instead of witnessing a sharp market crash, investors are seeing prolonged sideways movement and extended consolidation across global and Indian equities.

We are not the fastest growing economy, it's factually incorrect: Surjit BhallaIn an exclusive interview with India Today TV, prominent economist and former IMF executive director Surjit Bhalla discussed India's economic performance and the decline in foreign direct investment since 2015.

India-US partnership driven by trust and shared economic growth: Piyush GoyalSpeaking at the Annual Leadership Summit of the American Chamber of Commerce in India in New Delhi, Goyal said India has become a trusted partner for global businesses and is well-positioned to benefit from changing global economic trends.

AI saves 75% of my time: Anil Agarwal on why Indians should not fear new techWhat if one tool could save hours of your working day? According to Vedanta Chairman Anil Agarwal, technology is already doing exactly that. Sharing his own experience, he said it saves 75% of his time and urged Indians, especially youngsters, to embrace it rather than fear it.

Samsung chip workers could get bonuses worth nearly $400,000 amid AI boom: ReportUnder the proposed plan, bonuses will be closely linked to how well each business unit performs. Since Samsung's memory-chip business is currently benefiting from booming demand for AI memory products, employees in that segment stand to gain the most.
Sensex, Nifty erase early gains as RBI rate hike fears weigh on marketsThe BSE Sensex closed 135.03 points lower at 75,183.36, while the NSE Nifty50 slipped 4.30 points to end at 23,789.00.

Why the rupee's fall could make owning a home more expensive for youThe rupee's sharp slide is raising concerns that the RBI may need to raise rates. That could lift home loan EMIs and make buying a house harder for borrowers.

Rupee's fall sparks concern. But is the whole of Asia under pressure too?The rupee has extended its losing streak as a stronger dollar, costly crude and foreign outflows weigh on it. The sharper slide against several Asian peers has turned the move into a wider inflation and trade concern.

Goods transport costs rise from today, daily essentials may soon get costlierTransporters have introduced a fuel-linked surcharge as diesel prices climb. The move could push up logistics costs for groceries, medicines and online deliveries.

RBI announces $5 billion swap auction amid rupee weaknessIn a circular, the RBI said it had decided to conduct a USD 5 billion USD/INR buy-sell swap auction after reviewing current and changing liquidity conditions.

Silver slips by more than Rs 4,000: 3 key reasons behind the sharp fallIf you have been tracking silver prices, Thursday may have come as a surprise. Silver dropped by over Rs 4,000 on the MCX, even as global uncertainty remained high. Here are the three key factors behind the sharp fall.

India's power demand hits fresh record for 3rd straight day amid heatwaveThe country's peak power demand touched 265.44 gigawatts (GW) at 3:45 pm on Wednesday, going past Tuesday's previous high of 260.45 GW, according to data shared by the Ministry of Power.

Could the rupee ever hit 150 against the US dollar?Most economists will still rule out the viral prediction about the rupee hitting 150 against the US dollar. But in a world shaped by wars, oil shocks and volatile capital flows, is the debate really that absurd anymore?

Nvidia cashes in on AI gold rush with record earnings and $80 billion buybackNvidia Q1 results: The chip giant reported another blockbuster quarter, posting record revenue and profit numbers as global technology companies continue spending heavily on AI infrastructure, data centres and advanced computing systems.

'Melody' buzz keeps fuelling the wrong Parle on D-Street as stock jumps 5% againParle Industries shares hit an upper circuit again after videos showed Prime Minister Narendra Modi gifting Melody toffees to Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni. The move drew attention because the stock has no link to Parle Products, pointing to mistaken-identity buying.

Sensex, Nifty open higher as crude oil eases on hopes of Iran peace dealThe BSE Sensex jumped 345.02 points, or 0.46%, to 75,663.41 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 rose 131.30 points, or 0.55%, to 23,790.30 as of 9:44 am.

entertainment.headline: Jake Johnson wants to be Bollywood's hero: I want the dance number, everything Maximum Pleasure Guaranteed actors Jake Johnson and Jessy Hodges discussed playing morally complicated characters in an interview. The chat then veered into a playful but earnest pitch for an India-set project and a Bollywood dance number.

Karuppu box office Day 7: Suriya film smashes Rs 200 crore globally in 1st week Suriya and Trisha Krishnan's Karuppu has grossed Rs 207 crore worldwide in seven days. The film stayed steady on its first Thursday, with the Tamil version leading the run.

Pati Patni...box office Day 7: Film fails to beat predecessor's 1st week collection Pati Patni Aur Woh Do ended its first week in theatres with a gradual decline in collections across weekdays. The drop leaves the film trailing its predecessor and facing fresh pressure from new releases this Friday.

System review: Sonakshi Sinha, Jyotika's legal drama is sensitive but lacks impactRelease Date:Fri May 22, 2026 System movie review: Prime Video's System follows young lawyer Neha and court stenographer Sarika as they navigate a key case. Their bond gives the film a sharp lens on class, patriarchy and unequal access to justice.

Ronit Roy warns fans of cyber scam using his identity, shares details Ronit Roy has warned fans about scammers allegedly using his identity to contact people, especially women, and demand money for bookings and meetings with the actor.

Mohanlal's Georgekutty, the man who survived odds but lost himself by 3rd Drishyam Across the Drishyam films, Mohanlal's Georgekutty outsmarts the police while trying to protect his family. By the third film, that survival has left him broken by fear, lies and grief.

Drishyam 3 box office Day 1: Mohanlal's film is Mollywood's 2nd biggest opener Mohanlal's Drishyam 3 opened strongly in theatres on May 21 with Rs 43.37 crore worldwide. The Jeethu Joseph-directed thriller is now the second-biggest opener in Malayalam cinema after L2: Empuraan.

R Madhavan's GDN gets release date, makers reveal cast and new poster R. Madhavan's upcoming film, G.D.N., has locked its worldwide release date as the makers unveiled a new poster along with cast details. The biographical drama traces the life and legacy of inventor Gopalswamy Doraiswamy Naidu, known as the Edison of India.

Chand Mera Dil review: An eclipsed chaand dying a loud, overacted deathRelease Date:Fri May 22, 2026 Chand Mera Dil movie review: Ananya Panday and Lakshya's Chand Mera Dil turns love, marriage, feminism, trauma and toxic relationships into one long aesthetically-lit headache with missing chemistry and fully visible overacting.

Before you watch Mohanlal's Drishyam 3, here's a refresher on what happened so far Planning to watch Drishyam 3? With so much having already happened, here's recalling the adventures of Mohanlal's Georgekutty in the first two films.

Jyotika defends Suriya-Trisha's Karuppu amid criticism, says it has its own soul Actor-producer Jyotika defended her husband Suriya's latest film Karuppu amid criticism, saying the film has "its own soul" despite being a commercial entertainer. She added that Suriya's performance, especially in the final portions, has been widely appreciated alongside its box office success.

Ram Charan on intense body transformation for Peddi: Even lifting my daughter hurts Ram Charan talked about the intense body transformation he underwent for Peddi, moving between lean and bulky physiques for the role. He admitted the physical strain continues even after filming, saying simple actions like lifting his daughter have become painful.

Aishwarya Rai leaves for Cannes amid absence rumours, Aaradhya joins Aishwarya Rai departed from Mumbai to attend the Cannes Film Festival with her daughter, ending speculation about her absence. She greeted fans warmly despite rumours sparked by her exclusion from L'Oreal's campaign this year.
Emily in Paris to end with Season 6, filming begins in Greece Netflix has confirmed that Emily in Paris will end with its sixth season. The shoot for the season finale has begun in Greece.

Delhi court rules Ilaiyaraaja has no rights over En Iniya Pon Nilave song The Delhi High Court dismissed Ilaiyaraaja's appeal in the En Iniya Pon Nilave dispute with Saregama. The ruling said a composer controls the tune, but not the lyrics or the final recording.

Varun Dhawan's Hai Jawani… trailer launch cancelled over technical glitch The trailer launch of Varun Dhawan's Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai was cancelled at the last minute. The makers cited a technical glitch, while sources suggested the move was linked to a legal dispute over material involving Biwi No. 1.

Mohanlal celebrates birthday, cuts cake with fans on Drishyam 3 release day Mohanlal celebrated his 66th birthday at a Drishyam 3 screening after the film opened in theatres. Viral videos show the actor cutting a cake and waving at fans after the premiere.

Saba Azad on Hrithik Roshan's Storm, delay in Anurag Kashyap's Bandar release Actor Saba Azad opens up about the release of Anurag Kashyap's Bandar, working with Bobby Deol and Sanya Malhotra, and how Who's Your Gynac? sparked conversations around taboo topics.

Kim Soo-hyun cleared of allegations of dating Kim Sae-ron as a minor: Report South Korean actor Kim Soo-hyun has reportedly been cleared of underage dating allegations, while prosecutors now seek a YouTuber's arrest in a dramatic new twist to the controversy.

general news.headline: Govt admitted NEET breach. NTA chief says 'no leak': Does anyone know what happened?One arm of the government says there was a "breach in the chain of command". Another says the paper was not leaked "through the system". Between those two explanations lies the trust crisis surrounding India's biggest entrance exam.

2021 post-poll violence tweet returns to haunt Bengal actorsThe complaint cited a May 2, 2021 social media exchange between actors Parambrata Chatterjee and Swastika Mukherjee, alleging their remarks encouraged violence at a time when attacks on BJP workers were being reported across Bengal following the Assembly election results.

Bihar cracks down on banned Thai Mangur fish trade in statewide raidsBihar authorities are seizing and destroying Thai Mangur, a highly invasive and predatory fish species considered a threat to the environment, native fish populations and potentially human health.

Bandra slums demolition was decades overdue. But has Railways got the timing wrong?Western Railway demolished hundreds of houses and shops in Bandra's Garib Nagar after a court-backed clearance drive. A section of people backed the drive as long-overdue for safety and growing infrastructure needs. Others have criticised the timing as the displaced families will have no roof over their head during the monsoon.

Mohanlal's Georgekutty, the man who survived odds but lost himself by 3rd DrishyamAcross the Drishyam films, Mohanlal's Georgekutty outsmarts the police while trying to protect his family. By the third film, that survival has left him broken by fear, lies and grief.

Parle Industries hits 5% upper circuit again as investors chase wrong Parle stockParle Industries shares hit the upper circuit for a third straight session after the Modi-Meloni Melody video went viral. The rally reflected confusion over the Parle name, even though Melody toffees are made by unlisted Parle Products.

Illish finally beats Chingri again as East Bengal end 22 years of Bangal painAfter 22 years of inherited heartbreak, corporate chaos, and near-misses, East Bengal's dramatic ISL title triumph has revived a sleeping giant. The moshal burns bright again; Kolkata's red-and-gold half has finally reclaimed its soul.

System review: Sonakshi Sinha, Jyotika's legal drama is sensitive but lacks impactRelease Date:Fri May 22, 2026System movie review: Prime Video's System follows young lawyer Neha and court stenographer Sarika as they navigate a key case. Their bond gives the film a sharp lens on class, patriarchy and unequal access to justice.

Honda City facelift, Z-RV launch live updates: Price, features, design and moreAfter a long gap without any major product launches, Honda Cars India is set to introduce two new models today, the updated Honda City and the ZR-V.

Wrong turn, panic, no escape: How 5 Italian divers died inside Maldives caveFive Italian divers died during a deep-sea cave expedition in the Maldives after taking a wrong turn into a dead-end underwater chamber.

From love to tragedy: Twisha Sharma's wedding video surfaces as mystery deepensA wedding teaser featuring Twisha Sharma and Samarth Singh has surfaced as police investigate her death at her Bhopal matrimonial home. The video has added a poignant layer to a case that has sparked a massive controversy and a bitter fallout between the two families.

Found in Mumbai drain: Items for a fully furnished house, autorickshaw, police barricadeMumbai's pre-monsoon drain cleaning has pulled out household furniture, an autorickshaw and even a police barricade from eastern suburban nullahs this year. The recoveries show how Mumbaikars have turned the drains into landfills.

Cop turns to tantrik in theft probe, court steps in, orders change of officerA single-judge bench of Justice Munnuri Laxman also directed the Superintendent of Police, Nagaur, to transfer the investigation of a theft case to a sub-inspector-rank officer from another police station.

Mad rush for data centres, AI can spark fresh tech and job boom in IndiaBig tech companies are spending hundreds of billions of dollars on creating mega data centres that will fuel AI demand in the coming years. India, say experts, is well poised to benefit from the data centre boom, even if in some areas challenges remain.

Meta employee celebrates layoff, says her soul was dying; reveals next moveAsha Raval, who worked as a Product Designer at Meta since May 2022 according to her LinkedIn profile, was among roughly 8,000 employees impacted in the Mark Zuckerberg-led firm's latest round of layoffs.

Chand Mera Dil review: An eclipsed chaand dying a loud, overacted deathRelease Date:Fri May 22, 2026Chand Mera Dil movie review: Ananya Panday and Lakshya's Chand Mera Dil turns love, marriage, feminism, trauma and toxic relationships into one long aesthetically-lit headache with missing chemistry and fully visible overacting.

The cockroach that just won't go awayThe Cockroach Janta Party overtook BJP's Instagram followers' count, was blocked on X, and then came back. A lot happened throughout the day with the viral, satirical sensation that refuses to fade from public attention. Here's a recap.

I have a thing called Iran: Trump on attending son Don Jr's wedding amid warAs the US-Iran conflict deepens, President Donald Trump said he will try to attend son Don Jr's private Bahamas wedding this weekend, despite security concerns and mounting pressure.

Viksit Bharat 2047 is commitment, not slogan: PM after 4.5-hour meetThe high-level meeting, which started at around 5 pm on Thursday, continued for nearly four-and-a-half hours, with discussions centred on governance reforms, administrative efficiency and the long-term vision for "Viksit Bharat 2047".

India's beauty clinics under lens for risky injectable wellness dripsIndia's crackdown on injectable cosmetic products has uncovered a wider trade in unapproved wellness drips across clinics, salons and gyms. Dermatologists say the trend is being fuelled by off-label use, weak oversight and glossy marketing despite safety concerns.

ED raids across Bengal in extortion racket probe linked to ex-top copAccording to the ED, the extortion racket was allegedly run by Podder along with businessman Jay Kamdar, with investigators suspecting that former Kolkata DCP Santanu Sinha Biswas helped facilitate the network's operations.

Punjab man installs CCTVs on highway, sends Army movement feed to Pak handlersPunjab Police arrested a Pathankot man for allegedly installing CCTV cameras along NH-44 to monitor Army and paramilitary movement and share the surveillance feed with Pakistan-based handlers, officials said.

Snowfall in May: Unseasonal rain, snow turns dry Zojila whiteOn Thursday, fresh snowfall turned the area near Minamarg into a beautiful white landscape, even as temperatures soared in the plains below, highlighting how erratic India's weather has become.

sports.headline: Top 2 out of grasp, SRH hope to dent RCB's confidence in Hyderabad RCB vs SRH Preview: Defending champions RCB and SRH will play their final league-stage game in the IPL 2026. RCB are firmly placed at No.1 and SRH are unlikely to make it to the top 2 places due to their NRR deficit. So what's there to play for?

Illish finally beats Chingri again as East Bengal end 22 years of Bangal pain After 22 years of inherited heartbreak, corporate chaos, and near-misses, East Bengal's dramatic ISL title triumph has revived a sleeping giant. The moshal burns bright again; Kolkata's red-and-gold half has finally reclaimed its soul.

Season ends, enigma endures: CSK deliver final word on MS Dhoni's IPL future IPL 2026: After CSK's exit on Thursday, bowling coach Eric Simons said MS Dhoni alone will decide his IPL future despite missing the season with injuries. Suresh Raina's account of Dhoni saying 'let me see' keeps the retirement question alive.

IPL Play of the Day: Ahmedabad rings with Suii-raj as the Powerplay King rules IPL 2026, GT vs CSK: Mohammed Siraj produced a fiery new-ball spell in Ahmedabad that flipped the match on its head on Thursday. His burst of wickets and trademark celebrations set the tone for Gujarat Titans' dominant performance against the Super Kings.

Drums and tears: Ronaldo celebrates Saudi league title, his X post goes viral Cristiano Ronaldo scored twice as Al-Nassr won the Saudi Pro League title on Thursday in Riyadh. The 41-year-old teared up before celebrating his maiden triumph in Saudi with fans at Al-Awwal Park on Thursday.

Shubman Gill is IPL 2026's best captain: Ex-India star after GT crush CSK IPL 2026: Shubman Gill's captaincy credentials received massive backing after Gujarat Titans crushed CSK to seal a top-two finish push this season. Former India batter Mohammed Kaif called Gill the best captain of the season after another dominant GT display.

After roasting GT fielding, Gill leads by example with stunning CSK display Shubman Gill showed his team how things are done on the field by producing a brilliant run-out and catch during GT's win over CSK in Ahmedabad.

Ruturaj has no answer on Dhoni's future after CSK's 2026 exit Ruturaj Gaikwad said he does not know whether MS Dhoni will return after CSK's IPL 2026 exit. The uncertainty has sharpened focus on Dhoni's future as Chennai enter a transition phase.

Sai Sudharsan joins Virender Sehwag in elite IPL club with special fifty vs CSK IPL 2026: Sai Sudharsan's dream IPL 2026 season continued in Ahmedabad as the Gujarat Titans opener joined Virender Sehwag in an elite IPL batting club. The left-hander became only the second Indian ever to score five consecutive IPL fifties during GT's clash against Chennai Super Kings.

CSK crash and burn in Ahmedabad, exit IPL 2026 with 89-run loss to GT Chennai Super Kings suffered an 89-run loss to get eliminated from the IPL 2026 race as Gujarat Titans strengthened their position to finish in the top two. Gujarat's top three scored fifties while Rashid Khan, Mohammed Siraj and Kagiso Rabada picked up three wickets each to wreck the Chennai batting order.

Harry Maguire reacts to England World Cup snub, will Cole Palmer follow suit? Harry Maguire and Cole Palmer are among the notable players reportedly left out of England's World Cup squad by Thomas Tuchel, alongside other high-profile omissions. Meanwhile, several younger players including Kobbie Mainoo, Tino Livramento and Noni Madueke are set to be selected for the tournament.

Para archer Sheetal Devi's coach given jail term in sexual harrassment caseA Sonipat fast-track court sentenced India para archery coach Kuldeep Vedwan to five years in prison under the POCSO Act. Vedwan played a big role in Sheetal Devi's rise in para archery.

Prince Yadav's father reveals sacrifice behind India ODI call-upPrince Yadav's father recalled the sacrifices and tennis-ball tournaments behind his maiden India ODI call-up. The selection capped years of struggle and marked a major step in India's pace transition.

East Bengal clinch maiden ISL title, Mohun Bagan suffer front-row painTwenty-two years of hurt, chaos, collapses and heartbreak finally melted away for East Bengal on a wild Kolkata night. And to make it even sweeter for the red-and-gold faithful, the title arrived with Mohun Bagan falling behind them in the race that mattered most.

After Kohli's fitness remark, Manpreet invites RCB star for training challengeManpreet Singh has invited Virat Kohli and the RCB squad for a joint training session and yo-yo test. The exchange followed Kohli's admission that Indian cricketers are nowhere near hockey players in terms of fitness.

Rajat Patidar back in time for RCB's top-two push vs SRH, confirms Mo BobatIPL 2026: Mo Bobat confirmed Rajat Patidar is fit again and training before RCB's match against Sunrisers Hyderabad. His return sharpens Bengaluru's push for a top-two finish as Bobat also highlighted Bhuvneshwar Kumar's value across the dressing room.

National anti-doping act to be amended, jail term proposed for drug suppliersThe Centre has proposed amending the National Anti-Doping Act to criminalise the supply and trafficking of banned substances to athletes. The move hardens India's anti-doping stance as its poor WADA record collides with major hosting ambitions.

Rohit Sharma, Virat Kohli doing everything to last till 2027 World Cup: Ricky PontingRicky Ponting believes Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma are still pushing hard towards one final ODI World Cup run in 2027. While Kohli continues dominating with the bat, Rohit's biggest challenge may now be whether his body can keep up with another two-year cycle.

We are Champions daddy: How Arteta learnt Arsenal ended 22-year EPL waitArsenal sealed the Premier League title after Manchester City drew with Bournemouth. The moment ended a 22-year wait and allowed Arsenal to shift attention towards the Champions League final.

Afghanistan women's refugee team to tour England under ECB initiativeThe (ECB) will host an Afghanistan Refugee Women's Team for a tour beginning on June 22 under a special initiative aimed at supporting displaced Afghan cricketers and promoting inclusivity in the sport.

Why drop Hosein? CSK fans left fuming as spinner dropped for Short vs GTChennai Super Kings left out Akeal Hosein and brought in Matt Short for a must-win game against Gujarat Titans. The call jarred with supporters because Hosein had been one of CSK's more effective bowlers while the playoff equation remained unforgiving.

World Cup: Germany recall Manuel Neuer, 40, as first-choice goalkeeperGermany coach Julian Nagelsmann has brought veteran goalkeeper Manuel Neuer out of international retirement and reinstated him as the country's first-choice goalkeeper for the upcoming World Cup. The decision marks a major shift in Germany's plans after Oliver Baumann had been backed as the No. 1 following Euro 2024.

French Open 2026 draw: Jannik Sinner, Novak Djokovic learn about path to titleJannik Sinner and Novak Djokovic have landed on opposite sides of the French Open 2026 draw. That keeps open the prospect of a final between the top seed chasing a Career Grand Slam and Djokovic hunting a 25th major.

KKR star added to India A squad for Sri Lanka tri-series, replaces Harsh DubeyKKR star Anukul Roy has been drafted into the India A squad for the Sri Lanka tri-series in place of Harsh Dubey. The move rewards his strong KKR season as Anukul has been starter for Kolkata this season.

Nabi vs Brar: Why was one of India's best pacers not given Test debut?Jammu and Kashmir pacer Auqib Nabi guided his team to a historic Ranji Trophy victory this season. The incredible red-ball performance got him a white-ball IPL contract, but was not deemed good enough for the Indian Test team.

Novak Djokovic announces new head coach ahead of French Open: Who is Viktor Troicki?Novak Djokovic has appointed Viktor Troicki as his new head coach before the French Open. The move reunites two long-time Serbian allies as he chases a record 25th Grand Slam title.

Hardik Pandya sanctioned for misconduct during Mumbai Indians' loss to KKRHardik Pandya was fined 10 per cent of his match fee and received one demerit point after the Mumbai Indians captain forcefully dislodged the bails during the IPL 2026 match against Kolkata Knight Riders at the Eden Gardens on Wednesday.

Playing through pain: Varun refuses to take the easy option, says Shane WatsonKKR spinner Varun Chakravarthy should ideally be resting but simply refuses to give up on the team's campaign, said assistant coach Shane Watson. KKR have made an incredible comeback in the second half of the season after remaining winless for the first 6 games this season.

Beer, champagne and cigarettes? 1983 World Cup winner debunks viral party billFormer India cricketer Kirti Azad dismissed the viral restaurant bill linked to India's 1983 World Cup celebrations as fake, asserting that the team never visited the hotel mentioned in the receipt and that Kapil Dev's signature on the document had been forged.

East Bengal, Mohun Bagan headline four-way ISL title battle on dramatic final dayEast Bengal and Mohun Bagan headline a dramatic final day in the ISL title race, but Punjab FC and Mumbai City FC are still very much alive too. With four clubs separated by the finest margins, Thursday night could swing wildly with every goal across Kolkata and New Delhi.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
